requirement.description: The gateway shall increment a rolling message counter for all cyclic safety-related messages.

test_cases.test_objective: Verify that the GW_SafetyMessage contains a MsgCounter signal.
preconditions: CAN monitoring tool connected and configured to capture message ID 0x119
test_steps: Monitor the CAN bus for GW_SafetyMessage
Extract the MsgCounter signal from bits 32–35
test_cases.expected_result: MsgCounter signal is present in every GW_SafetyMessage frame.

test_cases.test_objective: Verify that MsgCounter increments by 1 for each consecutive GW_SafetyMessage.
test_cases.expected_result: MsgCounter increments by +1 between consecutive messages.

test_cases.test_objective: Verify that MsgCounter rolls over from 15 to 0.
test_cases.python_test_script:
def test_tc_gw_c04_003_counter_rollover(can_bus):
    msgs = collect_messages(can_bus, 0x119, 20)
    counters = [decode_msg_counter(m) for m in msgs]
    assert 15 in counters and 0 in counters

test_cases.test_objective: Verify that MsgCounter increments continuously without skipping values.
test_steps: Capture a sequence of GW_SafetyMessage frames
test_cases.expected_result: No skipped or repeated MsgCounter values observed.

test_cases.test_objective: Verify MsgCounter behavior after Gateway reset.
test_cases.expected_result: MsgCounter restarts from a valid value (0–15, typically 0).
test_cases.python_test_script:
def test_tc_gw_c04_005_counter_after_reset(can_bus):
    time.sleep(3)
    msg = collect_messages(can_bus, 0x119, 1)[0]
    assert 0 <= decode_msg_counter(msg) <= 15

test_cases.test_objective: Verify MsgCounter continues incrementing across ignition cycles.
test_cases.expected_result: MsgCounter resumes valid rolling increment behavior after ignition ON.

test_cases.test_objective: Verify MsgCounter is limited to 4-bit range (0–15).
Verify MsgCounter is present and increments in every transmitted GW_SafetyMessage.
test_cases.expected_result: Every GW_SafetyMessage contains a valid incrementing MsgCounter.
